title: Check Operator Station Fault Beacon To Identify A Tipper Fault
summary: Use the documented operator station fault beacon as an indicator when investigating a tipper fault associated with AGVs being directed to the hospital.
when_to_use: Use when investigating the troubleshooting condition described in the source as too many AGVs being directed to the hospital and the listed cause is a tipper fault. The source directs the user to look for the fault beacon on the operator station as the indication to check.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a complete tipper fault correction procedure because the supplied source section does not provide the corrective steps.
Do not use this runbook to infer additional recovery actions, commands, or HMI operations not explicitly provided in the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Go to the affected tipper operator station, inspect for the documented fault beacon indication, and record whether the source-backed indicator is present.

safety_notes: Use only the visual inspection and documentation actions supported by the source.
Do not invent tipper fault correction steps because the source section only states to correct the fault and return the tipper to service without providing the corrective procedure.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the affected operator station
Visual access to the operator station fault beacon

access_required: Access to the affected tipper operator station
system_preconditions: An affected tipper has been identified in the troubleshooting context of too many AGVs being directed to the hospital.

visual_references.description: Operator station HMI manual control screen artifact that can help orient the user to the operator station context referenced by the troubleshooting text.
Operator station screen artifact from a tipper-related recovery section; useful as supplemental operator station context only.

steps.title: Go to the affected tipper operator station
steps.purpose: Reach the operator station identified in the troubleshooting context so the documented indicator can be checked.
steps.instruction: Go to the operator station for the affected tipper associated with the AGVs being directed to the hospital.
steps.expected_result: You are positioned at the affected tipper operator station.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct operator station for the affected tipper is identified and accessible for inspection.
steps.failure_condition: The affected tipper operator station cannot be identified or accessed.

actions.label: Access affected operator station
actions.exact_action: Go to the operator station for the affected tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use as a reference for operator station context while locating the affected tipper operator station.

stop_or_escalate_if: The affected tipper cannot be determined from the available troubleshooting context.
The operator station cannot be accessed safely or its status cannot be inspected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Too many AGVs being directed to the hospital; cause: Tipper fault. Look for fault beacon on the operator station. Correct the fault. Return the tipper to service.

steps.title: Inspect the operator station fault beacon
steps.purpose: Check the specific source-backed indicator associated with the listed tipper fault.
steps.instruction: Look for the fault beacon on the operator station.
steps.expected_result: The beacon state is visually determined as present or not present.
steps.healthy_condition: The fault beacon can be clearly observed and its state can be determined.
steps.failure_condition: The fault beacon location or state cannot be determined from the operator station inspection.

actions.label: Look for fault beacon
actions.exact_action: Look for the fault beacon on the operator station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use as the closest available operator station visual reference in the packet while inspecting the operator station area for the fault beacon.

stop_or_escalate_if: The beacon cannot be located on the operator station.
The beacon state cannot be determined with confidence.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Look for fault beacon on the operator station.
The troubleshooting section states that a tipper fault associated with too many AGVs being directed to the hospital can be identified by looking for a fault beacon on the operator station.

steps.title: Use the beacon as the documented tipper fault indication
steps.purpose: Apply the source guidance that the operator station fault beacon is the indicator to check for this troubleshooting condition.
steps.instruction: Use the presence of the fault beacon as the documented indication associated with the tipper fault in this troubleshooting scenario.
steps.expected_result: The inspection result is interpreted using the source-backed indicator only.
steps.healthy_condition: The beacon observation is recorded as the source-supported indication for the suspected tipper fault.
steps.failure_condition: The observation cannot be tied to the documented troubleshooting condition or remains ambiguous.

actions.label: Interpret beacon indication
actions.exact_action: Use the presence of the fault beacon as the documented indication associated with the tipper fault.

stop_or_escalate_if: The beacon observation does not clearly support or rule out the documented tipper fault indication.
Additional diagnosis would require unsupported assumptions beyond the supplied source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Too many AGVs being directed to the hospital; cause: Tipper fault. Look for fault beacon on the operator station.

steps.title: Document limits of the source and escalate corrective action as needed
steps.purpose: Ensure the user does not exceed the source by inventing unsupported correction steps.
steps.instruction: Record that the source directs the user to correct the fault and return the tipper to service, but do not invent additional corrective steps from this section. If correction cannot be performed from other approved source-backed procedures available to your team, escalate for further support.
steps.expected_result: The finding is documented and unsupported corrective actions are avoided.
steps.healthy_condition: The beacon check result is recorded and any next action stays within approved source-backed guidance.
steps.failure_condition: The user attempts unsupported corrective action or cannot proceed because no source-backed correction is available.

actions.label: Document source limitation
actions.exact_action: Record that the source directs the user to correct the fault and return the tipper to service, without inventing additional corrective steps.

stop_or_escalate_if: The fault cannot be corrected from available source-backed procedures.
The beacon state cannot be determined.
Proceeding would require invented corrective steps not present in the supplied source.
